inputs.prompt: Programme: Morning News. Story: A primary school in a deprived neighbourhood has been rated Outstanding by inspectors — the first school in the area to receive the rating in a decade. The head teacher credits a focus on outdoor learning and community involvement. Parents are celebrating. Duration: 1:30. Reporter: Sophie Ward. Clips: Head teacher Maria Lewis (14 secs): 'We decided three years ago that our children would learn better if they spent more time outside the classroom. The inspectors saw what we see every day.' Union rep: No clip. Parent Aisha Begum (10 secs): 'My daughter comes home excited. She never used to. That tells me everything.'

Story: New cycle lanes causing traffic problems. Duration: 1:15. Reporter: Tom Harris. Clips: Council transport chief (12 secs): 'The data shows a 20% reduction in accidents on these routes.' Frustrated driver (8 secs): 'It took me 40 minutes to get to work today. It used to take 15.'

Programme: European Radio News. Story: Denmark has become the first EU country to trial a four-day working week in the public sector. The six-month pilot covers 12,000 civil servants. Early data shows productivity maintained but employee satisfaction up 23%. Critics say the model will not scale to the private sector. Duration: 2:00. Reporter: Anna Larsen. Clips: Danish employment minister (15 secs): 'We are not reducing work. We are rethinking how work is structured.' Business federation spokesperson (12 secs): 'Public sector conditions cannot simply be copied into competitive industries.' Civil servant participant (10 secs): 'I spend Fridays with my children now. I am a better employee on Monday because of it.'
